title: Rubber blends, articles thereof, and methods of making the same
abstract: Rubber blends including natural rubber and at least one propylene-based elastomer are provided herein. The rubber blends have good anti-aging properties as well as high gloss. Methods of making the rubber blends and articles made from the rubber blends, such as tires, are also provided.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS:  What is claimed is: 1. A rubber blend comprising: (i) a natural rubber present in an amount of about 40 to about 60 phr; and (ii) at least one propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 80 wt% propylene-derived units and about 5 to about 20 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the at least one propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 500 to about 8000 mPaÂ· s and a melting temperature of about 90Â°C to about 105 Â°C, and wherein the at least one propylene-based elastomer is present in an amount of about 1 to about 3 phr. 2. The rubber blend of claim 1, wherein the at least one propylene-based elastomer is a blend of: (a) a first propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 90 wt% propylene-derived units and about 2 to about 10 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the first propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 500 to about 2,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 92Â°C to about l02Â°C; and (b) a second propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 80 wt% propylene-derived units and about 5.0 to about 20 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the second propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 7,000 to about 8,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 95 Â°C to about l05Â°C. 3. The rubber blend of any preceding claim, wherein the rubber blend has a peel strength > about 10 kgf/cm. 4. The rubber blend of any preceding claim, wherein the rubber blend has one or more of the properties: a) a gloss measured at 20Â° of > about 30; b) a gloss measured at 60Â° of > about 65; and c) a gloss measured at 85Â° of > about 77. 5. The rubber blend of any preceding claim, further comprising at least one antioxidant in an amount of about 1 to about 6 phr. 6. The rubber blend of claim 5, wherein the at least one antioxidant is N-(l,3- dimethylbutyl)-Nâ-phenyl-p-phenylenediamine. 7. The rubber blend of any preceding claim, further comprising a wax in an amount of at most about 2 phr. 8. The rubber blend of claims 1-5, substantially free of a wax. 9. The rubber blend of any preceding claim, further comprising a filler present in an amount of about 35 to about 55 phr. 10. The rubber blend of claim 9, wherein the filler comprises carbon black. 11. The rubber blend of any preceding claim, further comprising a second rubber in an amount of about 40 to about 60 phr. 12. The rubber blend of any preceding claim, further comprising at least one additional agent selected from the group consisting of an oil, an accelerator, a curing agent, an anti scorch agent, a tackifier, a thermoplastic resin, and combinations thereof. 13. An article comprising the rubber blend of any preceding claim. 14. A rubber blend comprising: (i) natural rubber; (ii) at least one propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 80 wt% propylene-derived units and about 5 to about 20 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the at least one propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 500 to about 8,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 90Â°C to about 105 Â°C; 

 (iii) polybutadiene rubber; (iv) carbon black; (v) naphthenic oil; (vi) 2,2,4-trimethyl- 1 ,2-hydroquinoline; (vii) N-(l,3-dimethylbutyl)-Nâ-phenyl-p-phenylenediamine; (viii) thermoplastic resin; (ix) stearic acid; (x) zinc oxide; (xi) N-tertiarybutyl-2-benzothiazole sulfenamide; (xii) sulfur; (xiii) N-(cyclohexylthio)phthalimide; and (xiv) optionally, wax. 15. The rubber blend of claim 14, wherein the at least one propylene-based elastomer is a blend of: (a) a first propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 90 wt% propylene-derived units and about 2 to about 10 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the first propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 500 to about 2,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 92Â°C to about l02Â°C; and (b) a second propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 80 wt% propylene-derived units and about 5 to about 20 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the second propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 7,000 to about 8,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 95 Â°C to about l05Â°C. 16. The rubber blend of claims 14-15, wherein the rubber blend has one or more of the properties: a) a peel strength > about 10 kgf/cm; b) a gloss measured at 20Â° of > about 30; c) a gloss measured at 60Â° of > about 65; and d) a gloss measured at 85Â° of > about 77. 17. A method of producing a rubber blend comprising the steps of combining (a) about 40 to about 60 phr of natural rubber; and (b) about 1 to about 3 phr of at least one propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 80 wt % propylene-derived units and about 5 to about 20 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the at least one propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 500 to about 8,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 90Â°C to about l05Â°C and recovering a rubber blend. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the at least one propylene-based elastomer is a blend of: (a) a first propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 90 wt% propylene-derived units and about 2 to about 10 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the first propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 500 to about 2,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 92Â°C to about l02Â°C; and (b) a second propylene-based elastomer comprising at least about 80 wt% propylene-derived units and about 5 to about 20 wt% ethylene-derived units, by weight of the propylene-based elastomer, wherein the second propylene-based elastomer has a viscosity at l90Â°C from about 7,000 to about 8,000 mPa-s and a melting temperature of about 95 Â°C to about l05Â°C. 19. The method of claims 17-18, further combining one or more of the following to the natural rubber and the at least one propylene-based elastomer: (a) about 1 to about 6 phr antioxidant; (b) zero to about 2 phr wax; (c) about 35 to about 55 phr filler; and (d) about 40 to about 60 phr of a second rubber. 20. The method of claims 17-19, further comprising combining at least one additional agent selected from the group consisting of an oil, an accelerator, a curing agent, an anti scorch agent, a tackifier, a thermoplastic resin, and combinations thereof. 21. The method of claims 17-20, wherein the rubber blend has one or more of the following properties: a) a peel strength > about 10 kgf/cm; b) a gloss measured at 20Â° of > about 30; c) a gloss measured at 60Â° of > about 65; and d) a gloss measured at 85Â° of > about 77.
